content:
What protocol have you used to connect to the server in each application (imap or pop3)?

  • K9 Mail
  • Thunderbird Android
  • Thunderbird desktop on Mac

imap keeps client(s) and server synchnonised. Deletions performed on a client app are deleted on the server. The deletion will also be applied on any other clients connecting using imap.

Pop3 doesn't support synchronisation. It downloads new emails and stores them locally on the client / device. When you delete an email, only the local app's copy is removed.

It sounds like you have K9 setup to use POP3 and Thunderbird for Android using Imap.

You can check which protocol the mobile apps are using by navigating to ... Settings > account > fetching mail > incoming server

Server Name = Can very for imap and pop3 Port 993 = imap port 995 = pop3
creator: platform34

content:
Q1) Where does Thunderbird for Android save POP3 Mailboxes?

The mailbox data is stored within protected internal storage (app sandbox). It's not accessible unless you have a rooted phone.

Q2) Is there a Backup-Restore Feature for POP3 Mailboxes?

Thunderbird for Android doesn't currently offer any backup or restore mechanism for mailbox data.

You can add your idea(s) on Mozilla connect and receive votes from the community. The TB team take these into consideration when planning feature additions and roadmaps. https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/idb-p/ideas/label-name/thunderbird%20android

content:
Thunderbird for Android doesn't support OAuth with pop3 connections. I suspect your desktop is using POP3 and being imported on TB mobile via QR code.

Imap is the modern and recommended protocol.

To setup Imap

You should setup the gmail account manually, instead of using the QR code. If your still on the first time setup wizard, tap 'get started' and skip step 1 & 2.

  1. Tap the top left menu button to open the side bar.
  2. Tap settings > add account.
  3. Enter your email address
  4. Imap settings will be automatically found & configured
  5. Click the sign-in with google button. Login to the webpage.
  6. Grant TB Mobile permission to access your emails.

To remove an account.

  1. Tap the top left menu button to open the side bar.
  2. Tap settings.
  3. Tap the account name.
  4. Tap the 3 dot menu (top right).
  5. Tap remove account.

To continue using pop3

You will need to setup an app password via gmail's webmail settings page. https://support.google.com/mail/answer/185833?hl=en

Once you have the app password ...

Follow step 1 to 4 above to add the account.

  1. On the configuration found box, click the down arrow.
  2. Click edit configuration.
  3. Change protocol = Pop
  4. Change server = pop.gmail.com
  5. Change port = 995
  6. Change authentication = normal password.
  7. Enter your new app password in the password field.
  8. Click next to complete the account setup.

You can view and edit the configuration of an exiting account by navigating to ...

  • Settings > account name > fetching mail > incoming server
  • Settings > account name > sending mail > outgoing server

content:
Having only an outbox displayed occurs when the account's sign-in didn't complete successfully during setup. This can happen if the Oauth 2.0 account sign-in process was skipped or an unsupported configuration was imported.

Are the two accounts using ... 1. Imap or pop3 connections? 2. What authentication type is used on each. Oauth 2.0 or user name & password.

For the two accounts, can you navigate to 1. Settings > account > fetching mail > incoming server 2. Settings > account > Sending mail > outgoing server.

Click next on both screens to establish and test the server connection.

  • Port 993 = Imap, Port 995 = Pop3 on the incoming server page.
  • For oAuth authentication, you might be prompted to sign-in to your provider and grant the app permission to access your email account. Click the "sign-in with google" button as example.
  • Note that Thunderbird for Android doesn't support using OAuth with Pop3 connections. If this is the issue, you will need to create an app password via your webmail setting.

content:
Thunderbird for Android doesn't support the use of oAuth 2.0 with Pop connections. I suspect your desktop accounts are setup with this configuration and imported by QR code into the mobile app. This would explain the error you see.

If this is the case, you have two options.

  1. Continue using oAuth 2.0 authentication. You must change from Pop3 to an Imap connection.
  2. Continue using a Pop connection. You must ..

a. Generate an app password via your providers webmail settings. b. Change authentication type in TBA accounts from oAuth 2.0 to normal user name & password. c. Enter the newly generated app password in the password field.

Note: App passwords are a generated 16 digit passcode, not your normal email account password.

To check if Imap or pop is being used ... Navigate to settings > account name > fetching mail > incoming server.

Pop Server name = pop.gmail.com Port = 995

Imap Server name = imap.gmail.com Port = 993

Imap is the modern connection protocol, supporting sync on multiple devices.

Please screen shot any any errors and include with your response. If you use oAuth authentication and are prompted to sign-in, please do so and grant the app permission to access your emails.

4) Check server polling is enabled

  1. Navigate to settings > account name > fetching mail > folder poll frequency.
  2. Confirm a value is set. The default is 15 mins.

5) Check sync is enabled on folders.

  1. Open the side bar to show the account's folder list.

  3. Tap the inbox folder.
  4. Confirm 'Enable Sync' is toggles ON

content:
Hi Charalampos

The sync interval can't be reduced below 15 minutes. Google imposes this limit in Android for apps running background services such as TB's sync mechanism.

If you require for more timely notifications, you should enable the foreground push service. This is enabled per folder and provides immediate email delivery and notifications.

See the guide linked below. https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/configure-push-email-thunderbird-android

To receive notification of emails arriving in folders you've added.

1. Enable the Notifications setting on the folder. 2. Enable the Sync or push setting on the folder.

To access these settings ...

1. Open the side menu with account folders showing 2. Tap manage folders (bottom of list) 3. Tap desired folder name 4. Toggle above settings ON
